title: sidalcea neomexicana subsp. crenulata
intro: Sidalcea neomexicana subsp. crenulata, also known as New Mexico checkerbloom, is a plant species in the mallow family that is native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of glycosides and flavonoids. The health benefits of Sidalcea neomexicana subsp. crenulata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the dried flowers in a decoction to create a medicinal infusion.
